mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero, further policy easing cannot materially reduce discount rates, so equity returns over the next month are driven instead by improving growth and liquidity expectations; a stable or falling FEDFUNDS series therefore predicts positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with flat or declining FEDFUNDS still produce negative 20-day SPX returns in the majority of cases, or if the sign of the 20-day forward return is not better than chance within a near-zero rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals deteriorating household income and weaker future demand, which should pressure S&P 500 prices lower over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal loses directional accuracy in late-cycle slowdown windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand improvement in consumption and housing, which lifts earnings expectations and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upturns in retail sales and housing starts do not outperform single-series signals, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of joint-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO together with stable labor conditions implies output is expanding without an immediate inflationary shock, so equities should rally over the next quarter as cyclical revenues improve before costs reprice.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if production-expansion episodes with stable unemployment do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal fails once labor conditions stop confirming the industrial pickup.

mechanism_narrative: A rising GDPC1 trend confirms aggregate real demand is improving, and because equity cash flows are procyclical, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next quarter when GDP momentum is strong.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not correspond to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign agreement deteriorates over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays exceptionally low, real rates fall and monetary policy becomes more stimulative relative to inflation, which supports higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by > 2pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-rate and policy-rate divergence episodes fail to deliver positive 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are not stronger than when either proxy is used alone.

mechanism_narrative: A payrolls rebound in PAYEMS after a prior decline signals employment stabilization, which improves consumer income expectations and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as risk appetite recovers.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS turns up after at least 2 consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-rebound episodes do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal disappears when the labor market is stabilizing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in INDPRO and PAYEMS identifies a true production-and-labor contraction, which should weigh on earnings revisions and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both fall month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in industrial production and payrolls are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the combined signal adds no value beyond either series separately.
